name: South: Mangalore Pineapple Curry (Mangai Kari
recipeIngredient: Pineapple chunks (8 oz) packed in unsweetened juice
Curry leaves (10), or chopped fresh cilantro
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 8 to 10-inch skillet, dry-roast sesame seeds, fenugreek seeds, and coconut over medium heat for about 6 to 8 minutes until the seeds become aromatic and darken slightly. Transfer to a spice grinder and grind into a powder.
2. Combine pineapple chunks with juice, turmeric, brown sugar, salt, cayenne pepper, and ground spice mixture in a 1-quart saucepan. Cover and cook over medium heat for about 8 to 10 minutes until thickened.
3. Heat oil in a small frying pan over medium-high heat. Add mustard seeds; when they pop (about 1 minute), stir in curry leaves or cilantro. Remove from heat and pour the mixture over the pineapple curry.
4. Serve hot.
Per serving (4 servings): 70 calories, 1 g protein, 10 g carbohydrate, 3 g fat (1 g saturated), 0 mg cholesterol, 135 mg sodium, 1 g fiber.
